intro:
The best dating app matching algorithm in 2026 is the one that maximizes relevant connections and minimizes noise — not the one that maximizes time in app. Most major players optimize for engagement, not for your success.

Embir's algorithm optimizes for relevance: strict bidirectional orientation filtering, bidirectional intent matching, and declared value matching.

sections.title: Bidirectional — the real algorithmic differentiator
sections.content: On Tinder, Bumble, Hinge: you filter what YOU see, but not who sees you. Your profile can appear in feeds of people who don't match your orientation at all. Embir's algorithm applies constraints both ways: if you're gay, you don't appear to straight users AND straight users don't appear in your feed. This isn't a client-side preference — it's a constraint at the SQL query level.
highlights: Bidirectional = no orientation incompatibility slips through
Optimized for real connections, not screen time

verdict: In 2026, the best dating algorithm is the one that wastes the least of your time on incompatible profiles. Embir is built around this objective: bidirectional filtering, intent matching, verified profiles.
ctaText: The matching algorithm that respects your time — join Embir

faq.q: How does Embir's matching algorithm work?
faq.a: Embir uses strict bidirectional filtering: your orientation and intents apply both ways. You only see profiles for whom you're also a potential match.
Yes. Matching criteria are explicit: orientation, intent, values. No opaque black box.
Yes. Core connection features are free.
